name: G E A Employees
description_short: Gas & Electric Employees Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution in Mason City, Iowa, serving gas and electric workers and their families since 1932.
description_long:
Gas & Electric Employees Credit Union (GEECU) was founded in 1932 with just 81 members and $177.80 in initial shares. Over nine decades, it has evolved into a trusted community financial institution serving Mason City, Iowa, with over 400 members today. The credit union began with only three loans totaling $75 and has since expanded to offer a full range of financial services while maintaining its cooperative, member-focused mission.

GEECU offers comprehensive financial solutions including savings accounts, share certificates, loans, and home banking services. Members can access loan applications online, check current rates, and manage accounts through home banking platforms. The institution operates under a state charter in Iowa and is NCUA-insured, providing member protections standard to credit unions. Their service model emphasizes personalized financial guidance tailored to individual member needs.

What distinguishes GEECU is its deep historical roots in the gas and electric utility worker community and its commitment to that specific membership base and their families. The credit union maintains direct member engagement through annual meetings with social components (including the 2026 annual meeting with brunch) and limited but accessible office hours. Their longevity—over 90 years of continuous operation—demonstrates sustained trust and stability within their niche membership.

GEECU is best suited for current and former gas and electric utility workers in Iowa and their immediate families seeking personalized banking relationships. The primary limitation is their small size (400 members) and restricted service hours (Monday-Friday 10:30 AM–1:00 PM), which may present accessibility challenges for working members. Limited geographic reach and member base mean fewer amenities or services compared to larger credit unions, though this also reflects their focused, community-oriented mission.
services: Member annual meetings and community engagement
pros: Over 90 years of proven stability and member trust since 1932 founding
NCUA-insured member accounts providing federal protections
Personalized financial services tailored to individual member circumstances
Member-owned cooperative structure ensures not-for-profit, member-centric operations
Online home banking and loan application services for member convenience
Direct engagement with membership through annual meetings and community events
State-chartered institution offering comprehensive savings and loan products
cons: Very limited office hours (10:30 AM–1:00 PM Monday–Friday only) restrict accessibility
Small membership base (400 members) may limit service breadth and product offerings compared to larger institutions
Restricted to gas and electric workers and families, not open to general public
Geographic limitation to Mason City, Iowa area with single physical location
No information provided about specific loan products, rates, or eligibility requirements
best_for: Current and retired gas and electric utility workers seeking community-focused banking
Families of utility workers wanting personalized member service relationships
Iowa residents in the utility sector prioritizing financial institution stability and ethics
Members seeking local, cooperative credit union services over large corporate banks
diagnosis: Gas & Electric Employees Credit Union is best for utility workers and their families in Mason City, Iowa seeking a stable, member-owned financial institution with a 90+ year track record. The main caveat is severely limited accessibility due to restricted office hours and membership eligibility, making this practical only for local utility sector employees who can visit during daytime business hours Monday through Friday.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
